text.title: Zero-Click Search Adaptation Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Zero-Click Search Adaptation application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Optimize for being cited inside the AI Overview, not for being a skipped organic link; success metric switches from sessions to citation rate.
rule.rationale: In a world where 83% of AI Overview searches end without clicks, session-only measurement misses the actual brand-awareness value delivered.

rule.statement: Article + FAQPage JSON-LD are mandatory on every targeted page; pages without valid structured data are rejected.
rule.rationale: Studies show +28% AI citation rate for pages with proper structured data; AI crawlers parse JSON-LD to assess authority.

rule.statement: First paragraph MUST be a direct 40-60 word answer to the target query — no preamble, no marketing intro.
rule.rationale: AI Overviews lift verbatim from the lede; preamble dilutes citation signal.

rule.statement: Every targeted page MUST contain ≥3 original data points with explicit year + source; recycled stats without dates are rejected.
rule.rationale: Fresh original research with current statistics is cited 3.2x more than aggregated content.

rule.statement: KPI set MUST include impressions, branded queries, AI citation rate, and on-SERP actions — sessions alone is rejected.

rationale.source: Google Search Central — Search Console docs

rule.rationale: Session-only measurement in a zero-click environment produces false negatives that trigger premature reversals.

rule.statement: Verdicts on citation strategy MUST wait at least 60 days; 30-day judgments are rejected as premature.
rule.rationale: AI citation rate is lagging by 60-90 days; short-window verdicts produce false negatives.
